functions.author: Kael'Vorr
functions.title: The Flesh Without Form
pages:
The Flesh Without Form

Notes on Flesh Pods, living blood, and growth without a body.

Origin

Kael'Vorr found a dimension where flesh survived after every animal had vanished. It no longer remembered what shape it belonged to.

The Pod

A Flesh Pod is an answer without a question: tissue that grows, feeds, and protects itself without organs or identity.

Arrival

He transported a single sample to the Overworld. Stable soil gave it structure; rain gave it reach; nearby life gave it examples.

Not a Plant

It may root and spread, but it is not vegetation. It imitates the useful behavior of plants while remaining living flesh.

Blood Growth

Blood blocks, flesh blocks, vines, and roots are parts of one adaptable system. Separate them and each tries to become the whole.

Flesh Pod Behavior

Pods defend their growth and supply Blood Meat. Approach as though the ground itself has noticed you.

Blood Meat

It can be cooked in a furnace, smoker, campfire, or soul campfire. Cooking makes it safer, not less unsettling.

Chaos Material

Living flesh repairs itself. Kael'Vorr uses that property in Chaos equipment and the Coffin Backpack.

Coffin Backpack

At a Tier IV Chaos Altar, combine demon flesh, a Demon Heart, Demon Steel, a chest, and blood-flesh material.

Chaos Keys

A rare Flesh Pod may hold a Chaos Key. Kael'Vorr enjoys placing doors inside things that should not have pockets.

Containment

Fire slows growth. Clean borders help. Curiosity is not containment.

Kael'Vorr's Note

A body is merely flesh agreeing on a direction. I removed the agreement.
